recording_notes: Phone filming RCA monitor — same skills lab as V11-V14/V17 (pegboard, FLS FUNDAMENTALS manual, scissors visible). Pink/red foam block. Black suture. Heavy suture debris on tape from many prior attempts.

frame_analyses.description: At 10 seconds, both instruments at drain. Black suture visible. Needle loading/early placement.
At 50 seconds, STILL in suture placement. Both instruments near drain. Needle driving in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Very extended placement — still placing at 50 seconds. Among the worst placement times (V10: 67s, V11: 50s).

frame_analyses.description: At 99 seconds, second throw in progress. Both instruments at drain with suture crossing. Knot forming.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Throw sequence proceeding at moderate pace. Second throw at ~99 seconds.

frame_analyses.description: At 159 seconds, cutting phase. Both instruments at drain. Suture tails and loop visible extending upward. Knot X pattern visible. Extended cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting phase well underway but far from complete at 159 seconds.

frame_analyses.description: Final frame at 198 seconds. Instruments withdrawn. Knot visible with dark suture cross pattern, short tails. Drain slit appears CLOSED — no obvious gap. Drain not avulsed, centered on block.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 193-second completion — matches pre-training baseline (198s). Despite slow time, the drain closure quality appears good. WORST cutting time in the series at ~65 seconds.

knot_assessments.notes: Extended third throw at 28s. Confidence low.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Despite very slow time, drain closure appears good from monitor view. No visible gap. This suggests the trainee may have been working more carefully/slowly but achieving better closure.

technique_summary: V19 at 193 seconds is a severe regression — the slowest since V10 (215s) and essentially matching the pre-training baseline (198s). Cutting at 65 seconds is the WORST EVER in the series (previous worst: 55s in V16 and V10). Suture placement at 43 seconds is also very extended. The throws at 73 seconds total are the slowest in the post-V11 series. Paradoxically, the drain closure appears good despite the extremely slow time — this may indicate the trainee was prioritizing quality over speed, or severe fatigue caused deliberate, slow movements. If this followed V17/V18 in the same session, this is a classic late-session fatigue crash.
improvement_suggestions: STOP PRACTICING if this was attempt #4+ in the same session — this is severe fatigue
Cutting at 65 seconds is catastrophic — more than double the V14 best (29s). This single phase accounts for 34% of total time
Session discipline is the #1 priority: cap at 2-3 attempts maximum
When rested, the trainee can achieve 107-135 seconds — this 193s time does not reflect true ability
strengths: Drain closure appears good despite severe time regression
Drain not avulsed — safe tissue handling maintained even when fatigued

key_observations: 193s / 402 FLS — matches pre-training baseline, 86s slower than best (V14: 107s)
Placement 43s + Cutting 65s = 108s (56% of total time in just two phases)
Throws 73s total — slowest post-V11, even throws affected by fatigue
Good drain closure despite slow time — quality maintained under fatigue
Classic late-session fatigue crash: V18 (145s) → V19 (193s) = 48-second drop
